memory.summary: I now know I have a free afternoon and can attend leisure_now. Since my term project is barely started, this relaxing option seems like a reasonable way to manage my low energy.
Though I previously planned to relax, my term project is critically behind schedule. Working now prevents future crunch and reduces Thursday stress.
My term project progress doubled in two hours, yet my stress and sleep debt remain high. The recent work spike confirms the project is still critically behind schedule.
After two hours of work, my project progress increased by 0.14, but my stress and sleep debt remain high despite the effort.
After 2 hours of focused work, my project progress improved from 0.61 to 0.70, yet my high stress and sleep debt persist despite the effort.
I failed a trivial chore while making term project progress, confirming both tasks are behind schedule despite my recent effort.
Worked 2 hours on the term project, raising progress to 0.86, but stress and sleep debt remain high with only 3 days left.
Progress jumped to 0.94, but with only 3 days left and high stress/sleep debt, the remaining time feels insufficient to finish.
The term project is now complete, but my high stress and sleep debt mean I need to prioritize rest immediately.
The term project is complete, yet my energy dropped to 2.4 after only 2 hours of sleep. I must prioritize rest immediately due to high stress and insufficient time remaining.
The project is finished, confirming my earlier concern about insufficient time. I must now prioritize rest to manage my high stress and sleep debt.
The term project is finished. With high stress and no sleep debt, I should rest now.
